commit.message:
🐛 fix: template all modular gemfiles in preflight

Replace narrow BOOTSTRAP_MODULAR_GEMFILES list + empty-stub fallback
with a full recursive walk of the installed template directory.

Previously, only templating.gemfile and templating_local.gemfile were
written in the pre-bundled phase; every other modular gemfile received
an empty stub. Empty stubs caused `bundle install` to fail with
`cannot load such file -- rubocop/rake_task` (and similar) when the
Rakefile tried to require gems that were absent from the bundle.

New behaviour:
- preflight_merge_modular_gemfiles! globs all *.gemfile.example files
  recursively under the installed template/gemfiles/modular/ tree.
- Each file is token-resolved and merged (or written fresh) into the
  destination repo before bundle install runs.
- The BOOTSTRAP_MODULAR_GEMFILES / BOOTSTRAP_FORCEABLE_MODULAR_GEMFILES
  constants are removed as no longer needed.
- preflight_ensure_eval_gemfile_stubs! and ensure_bootstrap_modular_gemfiles!
  are removed (dead code whose job is now done by the glob-based walk).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Install python3-ijson from Debian repos for route_check performance (#26633)

PR #4294 forced the pure-Python ijson backend in route_check.py to avoid
yajl2_cffi crashes under monit's MemoryDenyWriteExecute=true. However, the
python backend is slow, causing a regression on scaled platforms (21 min for
100K routes on Nokia chassis).

Root cause: sonic-yang-mgmt and sonic-yang-models pin ijson==3.2.3 (exact),
forcing pip to download from PyPI even when Debian's python3-ijson (3.4.0,
with the fast yajl2_c C extension) is installed. The PyPI wheel for Python
3.13 lacks the _yajl2 C extension, and pip's copy at /usr/local/lib/ shadows
the Debian package at /usr/lib/python3/.

Fix:
- Relax ijson==3.2.3 to ijson>=3.2.3 in sonic-yang-mgmt and sonic-yang-models
- Install python3-ijson via apt before pip installs sonic-yang-mgmt, so pip
  sees Debian's 3.4.0 and skips the PyPI download entirely

With this change, ijson auto-detects the yajl2_c backend (24x faster than
the python backend, safe under MemoryDenyWriteExecute).

Fixes: sonic-net/sonic-buildimage#23442

Signed-off-by: Deepak Singhal <deepsinghal@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Deploy themselves as K8s jobs
- List pods and jobs in namespaces
- Delete completed jobs
- Fetch pod logs

   all sessions on startup. Frontend now detects stale sessions and
   offers Resume/New Session choice instead of silently attaching to
   a dead session. Resume endpoint now accepts Terminated status.

   from the statusDot span — the CSS circle is sufficient. No more

   deletes the file if it was created by the session. Prevents
   unbounded growth and .github directory deletion.

* fix: prevent flaky ResourceSampling test from CPU measurement spikes (#192)

When SampleAllPluginsAsync is called back-to-back (as tests do),
wallClockMs between calls is near-zero, producing astronomically high
CPU percentages. This can trigger IsAggregateLimitExceeded(), suspending
the plugin before violations reach the warning threshold — causing
PluginResourceLimitEvent to never be published.

Changes:
- Initialize _lastProcessCpuTime and _lastSampleTime in constructor
  (not just ExecuteAsync) so the first sample has a proper baseline
- Add MinSampleIntervalMs (10ms) guard on CPU calculation to prevent
  unrealistic spikes from sub-millisecond elapsed times
- Lower test MaxMemoryMb from 16 to 1 for extra robustness

commit.message:
fix: three bugs — check-in data loss, button nesting, date hydration

- dance/page.tsx CheckInTab: add useEffect([existing?.id]) to sync
  scores + reflection state when localStorage data arrives after mount.
  Root cause: useState initializes once; on first render checkIns=[]
  so existing=undefined and all scores defaulted to emptyScores().
  After the parent useEffect loaded saved data, props updated but
  local state stayed empty. Fix: sync once when existing.id appears.

- dance/page.tsx History tab: change inner Edit <button> (line 871)
  to <div role='button'> — buttons cannot be descendants of buttons,
  which caused a React hydration error.

- daily/page.tsx: move todayDateStr computation from module level
  into the component via useState('') + useEffect, computed with
  new Date() on the client only. Eliminates the SSR/client date
  mismatch (server rendered Apr 3, client rendered Apr 9).

safeExpression: block loop, with, throw, debugger, and try statements (#3874)

* safeExpression: block loop, with, throw, debugger, and try statements

Loop statements (while, do-while, for, for-in, for-of) inside arrow
function callbacks could pass AST validation, enabling infinite loops
that block the event loop. Also block with (scope manipulation), throw
(disrupts caller), debugger (pauses execution), and try (swallows
exceptions).

🐛 fix: recurse into empty flow mappings for add_template_only_nodes

When a destination YAML mapping has an empty flow value (e.g. `files: {}`),
`can_merge_recursively?` was returning false because `flow_mapping?` detected
the key and value on the same line.  As a result, template-only child nodes
(e.g. `AGENTS.md: strategy: accept_template`) were never added, causing
downstream consumers like kettle-jem to fall back to the default :merge
strategy for those files.

Changes:
- Add `empty_flow_mapping?` private helper that returns true when a node is
  a flow mapping whose value carries no entries.
- In `can_merge_recursively?` allow recursion when dest is an empty flow
- In `emit_recursive_merge` skip the dest key line (which would emit the
  literal `{}`) for empty flow mappings and use the template key line
  instead, so child entries are emitted as valid block YAML.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 1 — Drop-in activation layer:

- Auto-enable via GFXGRAPH=1 env var

- Monkey-patches torch.cuda.CUDAGraph transparently

Phase 2 — Fix 5 blocking bugs (from adversarial review):
- conditional_bridge: Replace broken supergraph+NodeSetEnabled with
  per-branch exec dispatch (hipGraphNodeSetEnabled unsupported on
  child graph nodes in HIP 7.2)
- launch_pipeline: Fix wrong-exec update + shared-memory corruption.
  Single stream serialization, event-tracked state machine, updates
  target exec[active] (next-to-launch, not in-flight)
- launch_by_flag: hipStreamSynchronize before DtoH copy, strict
  error on out-of-range (no silent clamping)
- shape_manager: Validate strictly ascending bucket order + positive
  values, enforce HGB_MAX_BUCKETS=64 cap
- capture_compositor: Validate deps (no forward/self refs)

Additional hardening (from rubber-duck reviews):
- init.cpp: std::atomic + std::mutex, runtime ROCm version detection,
  current device (not hardcoded 0), hgb_is_initialized() API
- Device affinity: stored + validated on all launch/update/destroy
- Destroy paths: sync owning device before freeing resources
- Pipeline: conservative sync on hipEventRecord failure
- All source: init guard on every public function entry point
